title: poa wardiana
intro: Poa wardiana, also known as Mountain Timothy Grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the alpine regions of New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues and reducing fever, due to its expectorant and antipyretic properties. Poa wardiana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
